feed.title: Phonetica - International Journal of Phonetic Science
feed.subtitle: Latest articles from Phonetica - International Journal of Phonetic Science (De Gruyter)

entry.title: Articulatory adaptations to the effect of nasality on vowel formants in Xinfeng Tieshikou Hakka
entry.summary: Authors: Qian Zhang, Jason A. Shaw DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/phon-2025-0052 Pages: Page range: 125-150

entry.title: Variation and change in the production of te reo Māori closing vowel sequences
entry.summary: Authors: Kirsten Culhane, Jennifer Hay, Simon Todd, Márton Sóskuthy, Jeanette King, Forrest Panther, Peter J. Keegan DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/phon-2025-0043 Pages: Page range: 151-206

entry.title: Recognizing vocal emotions in two unfamiliar languages: universal patterns versus cultural and prosodic proximity
entry.summary: Authors: Yachan Liang, Vincent J. van Heuven, Roeland van Hout DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/phon-2025-0046 Pages: Page range: 207-243

entry.title: From ‘preferred’ to ‘peripheral’? A century-long study of linking-r in Standard Southern British English (1920s–2020s)
entry.summary: Authors: Jose A. Mompean DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/phon-2025-0037 Pages: Page range: 245-283

entry.title: Gradient boundaries through confidence intervals for forced alignment estimates using model ensembles
entry.summary: Authors: Matthew C. Kelley DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/phon-2025-0039 Pages: Page range: 285-305
